g.text: Rollback Journal — Write Ordering & Crash Safety
INVARIANT: Journal entry must reach physical disk BEFORE any DB page is modified

text.tspan: The two fsync barriers (③ and ⑤) enforce write ordering.
Without ③: DB could be written before journal is durable — crash loses undo info — CORRUPT.
Without ⑤: Journal could be deleted before DB write is durable — data silently lost after COMMIT.

g.text: DB File: UNCHANGED — zero writes issued

text.tspan: ✓ SAFE — No journal found on restart
DB is already consistent, nothing to undo
Creates journal file on first write
Writes to OS page cache (not yet durable)

g.text: DB File: UNCHANGED — write never reached DB

text.tspan: ✓ SAFE — Invalid journal discarded on restart
DB was never touched; no restore needed
Original page is now PHYSICALLY DURABLE
on the storage medium

g.text: DB file write is FORBIDDEN until this returns
DB File: UNCHANGED — no DB write yet issued

text.tspan: ✓ SAFE — Restore from journal (effective no-op)
DB page matches original; journal deleted

g.text: Overwrites page at correct file offset in DB
TORN PAGE risk: sector size < page size

text.tspan: DB File: TORN PAGE
(first sectors new, last sectors old)
✓ SAFE — Restore from journal
pwrite(original_page) → torn page overwritten
Modified page is now PHYSICALLY DURABLE
on the storage medium

g.text: Journal deletion FORBIDDEN until this returns
DB File: Modified page DURABLE on disk

text.tspan: ✓ SAFE — Journal found; restore original
Tx rolls back (user never saw COMMIT return)
No journal = COMMITTED
Journal exists = INCOMPLETE

g.text: DB File: Modified page DURABLE and CURRENT

text.tspan: ✓ DURABLE — Transaction fully committed
No recovery action on restart
